_comment: Scheduling metadata for the CRUD scenarios in scenarios.json. Keeps service prerequisites and account quota kinds as DATA so a scheduler can serialize/parallelize lifecycles safely against core.budgets (vpc=5, private-dns=3, ...). 'quota_kinds' maps a lifecycle id -> the capped resource kinds it CREATES (and must reserve in core.budgets before running); a scheduler should serialize lifecycles that share a kind once the live usage + reserved slots reach the limit. 'prerequisites' lists the in-flow resource families each lifecycle stands up before its headline resource (informational: these are created inline by the lifecycle's own steps, not external preconditions). 'budget_paths' maps the create PATH that consumes a kind -> the kind, mirroring engine._budget_kind_for_path so the mapping has one source of truth.

vpc_schedule._comment: Strategy to keep runs from colliding on the account VPC cap (=5). See knowledge/vpc-scheduling-strategy.md for the full rationale + next-run playbook. 'per_run_vpc_cap' is the max concurrent live VPCs a single run should hold (5 minus headroom for async-delete linger). 'lanes' partition the eight VPC-consuming lifecycles into non-overlapping shards; run each lane as a SEPARATE, non-overlapping workflow_dispatch (allow_mutations+allow_destructive; heavy lanes also run_heavy) via crud_filter, waiting for each run's regression job to finish before starting the next. 'cross_run_policy' = how to stop concurrent runs from competing for VPCs. 'durable_fix' = the code change that makes a single un-sharded full run safe (then lanes are optional).
vpc_schedule._classification_comment: Shared-infra parallel design (knowledge/vpc-scheduling-strategy.md). 'adopt_lifecycles' reuse the ONE session-shared VPC+subnet (provisioned once by regression.scenarios.shared_infra --provision; ids passed via SCP_SHARED_VPC_ID/SCP_SHARED_SUBNET_ID env) and create only their own child resource -> safe to run in PARALLEL with pytest-xdist. 'vpc_crud_lifecycles' MUST self-create (their purpose is vpc/subnet/peering creation, they MUTATE the shared vpc, or they need >1 vpc) -> run SERIALLY (xdist -n0) so they stay within per_run_vpc_cap (1 shared + <=1 self-created at a time). shared_infra.py --print-filters derives PARALLEL_K = 'not (<vpc_crud ids OR'd>)' and VPC_CRUD_K = '<vpc_crud ids OR'd>' so the two -k selections partition all enabled CRUD lifecycles with no overlap.

fixed_ip_map._comment: ADOPT-class lifecycles re-home their fixed host IPs into the shared subnet (10.124.0.0/24) at DISTINCT, non-overlapping host addresses so two parallel adopters never claim the same IP in the ONE shared subnet. Reserve .1 (gateway). All these steps are optional+broad expect_status so a reject still records coverage.

vpc_schedule.cross_run_policy: Never let two VPC-mutating runs overlap. Preferred: a single shared concurrency group for mutating runs (cancel-in-progress:false) so they queue. Interim: trigger one lane at a time and wait for the prior run's regression job to complete (the reliable 'VPCs no longer being created' signal) before the next.
vpc_schedule.shared_vpc_adopt: IMPLEMENTED (shared VPC + shared SUBNET, parallel-adopt): the 10 adopt_lifecycles carry {'adopt':'vpc'} on create-vpc/delete-vpc AND {'adopt':'subnet'} on create-subnet/delete-subnet, reusing ONE session-shared VPC (10.124.0.0/20) + ONE shared subnet (10.124.0.0/24) provisioned by engine.provision_shared_vpc. Ids flow via SCP_SHARED_VPC_ID/SCP_SHARED_SUBNET_ID env so every pytest-xdist worker adopts the SAME live infra. ADOPT-class fixed host IPs were re-homed into the shared subnet at distinct addresses (see fixed_ip_map) to avoid collision under parallel runs. Self-create stays as fallback when no shared id is present. The 7 vpc_crud_lifecycles self-create (run serially). See tests/crud/test_shared_vpc_adopt.py + knowledge/vpc-scheduling-strategy.md.
vpc_schedule.durable_fix: Remaining (to make a single un-sharded run fully self-regulating): (1) share ONE core.budgets.Budget across the pytest session and Budget.sync('vpc'/'private-dns', live_count) from a real list call before creates (the pytest driver currently builds a fresh Budget per lifecycle and never syncs, so the reserve gate sees a phantom 5 free); (2) drain-wait on GET /v1/vpcs after a VPC teardown before the next VPC create; (3) reorder so heavy-shared-networking is not last, or rely on (1)+(2).

notes: Quota kinds come from core.budgets.DEFAULT_LIMITS (vpc=5, private-dns=3). networking-dns-hosted-zone is currently disabled (needs a private-dns activate sub-flow) and so carries no quota row yet; when re-enabled it should declare private-dns. The four heavy lifecycles each stand up their own VPC inline, so under the 5-VPC cap a scheduler must serialize them (and the two light VPC lifecycles) rather than run them concurrently. The engine reserves a slot in core.budgets before each /v1/vpcs (and /v1/private-dns) create and releases it on teardown; a multi-process scheduler should first Budget.sync() each kind from a live list call, then gate concurrency on Budget.available(kind).
